nameMeanings.name: Methley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from Methley (WR Yorks).

variants.name: Medley
meanings: English: variant of Methley, a habitational name from Methley (Yorkshire). The placename may derive from Old English middel ‘middle’, replaced by the Old Norse cognate methal, + Old English lēah ‘woodland clearing’ or ēg ‘island’, or from Old English mǣth ‘mowing, the cutting of grass or corn’ + lēah. Topography suggests that Old English middel + ēg is the most likely origin.

en.lifeSummary: Methley was born in 1204, in Methley, West Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. He married Mrs Methley before 1230, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter.
